remarks.name: Tigera Blog (Calico / AI Security)
remarks.role: AI agent governance, security, accountability frameworks, enterprise AI deployment policy.
remarks.text: Five Principles of an Accountable AI Agent Network: How to Evaluate Any Governance Platform
remarks.content: The first post in this series argued that AI agent governance hasn’t kept pace with deployment. The second laid out the five pillars of accountability, and what is required. The third walked through why network... The post Five Principles of an Accountable AI Agent Network: How to Evaluate Any
remarks.contentTranslation: 本系列的第一篇文章认为，AIAgent治理没有跟上部署的步伐。第二部分列出了问责制的五大支柱，以及需要做些什么。第三部分讲解了为什么要建立网络……《负责任的AIAgent网络的五项原则：如何评估
remarks.translation: 负责任的AIAgent网络的五大原则：如何评估任何治理平台

remarks.content: You know every service in your cluster by name. You know which team owns each one, what it talks to, how it scales, where its logs go. The agents are a different story. That’s not... remarks.name: Dataiku Blog
remarks.role: AI governance, enterprise AI platform deployment, MLOps, analytics, agent orchestration.
remarks.text: Enterprise AI transformation: how leading organizations are redesigning operations around AI
remarks.content: Most enterprises have run AI pilots. But few have transformed their operations with AI at scale. What separates them is organizational design, not technology: disconnected people, disconnected systems, disconnected governance. According to "Global AI confessions report: data lead
remarks.contentTranslation: 大多数企业都运行AI试点。但很少有人用AI大规模地改变了他们的运营。将它们区分开来的是组织设计，而不是技术：断开的人员、断开的系统、断开的治理。根据《全球AI认罪报告：数据线索
remarks.translation: 企业AI转型：领先组织如何围绕AI重新设计运营
remarks.observation: 这条言论适合观察 builder 对产品、创业、销售或市场节奏的判断；可作为选题线索，不直接等同于行业结论。

remarks.content: Partnerships are often judged too early, or on the wrong things. Visibility. Executive alignment. Event presence. A well-worded press release. None of that is the real test.
remarks.contentTranslation: 人们往往过早地判断伙伴关系，或者判断错误。可见性。高管一致性。事件存在。措辞良好的新闻稿。这些都不是真正的考验。

remarks.name: Dataiku Blog
remarks.role: AI governance, enterprise AI platform deployment, MLOps, analytics, agent orchestration.
remarks.text: Alteryx data analytics in 2026: strengths, limitations, and alternatives
remarks.content: In our view, Alteryx built its reputation on a simple promise: Let business analysts do data work that used to require SQL, Python, or a request to IT. For years, that promise held. Drag-and-drop workflows replaced manual spreadsheet processes. Teams that waited weeks for data engineerin
remarks.contentTranslation: 我们认为， Alteryx的声誉建立在一个简单的承诺之上：让业务分析师做过去需要SQL、Python或IT请求的数据工作。多年来，这个承诺一直保持着。拖放工作流取代了手动电子表格流程。等待数据工程师数周的团队
remarks.translation: 2026年Alteryx数据分析：优势、局限性和替代方案

remarks.name: Garry Tan
remarks.role: President & CEO ycombinator —Founder garryslist—Creator of GStack & GBrain—designer/engineer who helps founders—SF Dem accelerating the boom loop
remarks.text:
The centerpiece stat is a self-own: she cites a 35-year study where 12.3% of 677 gifted kids reached "eminence" (full professor, Fortune 500 exec, federal judge) as proof the category is a lie. 

The general-population base rate for outcomes that rarefied is far worse, much closer to zero than double digit percentage. 12.3% is the selection mechanism working spectacularly.
remarks.translation: 核心数据是自我拥有：她引用了一项为期35年的研究，其中677名有天赋的孩子中有12.3 ％达到了“卓越” （正教授，财富500强高管，联邦法官） ，以证明该类别是谎言。稀疏结果的总体人群基本率要差得多，比两位数百分比更接近于零。12.3%的选择机制非常有效。
remarks.observation: 这条言论适合观察 builder 对产品、创业、销售或市场节奏的判断；可作为选题线索，不直接等同于行业结论。

remarks.text:
The community note clearly specifies the writer Katie Arnold-Ratliff is the one whose understanding of gifted is inherently flawed

Stop making it cool for writers to in-group signal with lies https://t.co/QSxcjimgYa
remarks.translation: 社区说明明确指出，作家Katie Arnold-Ratliff对天赋的理解本质上是有缺陷的。停止让作家冷静下来，用谎言发出群体信号
remarks.observation: 这条言论适合观察 builder 对产品、创业、销售或市场节奏的判断；可作为选题线索，不直接等同于行业结论。

remarks.name: Swyx
remarks.role: achieve ambition with intentionality, intensity, integrity & insanity.
remarks.text:
## On Loopcraft

One might argue the entire game of the next century is to be able to stack loops as effectively as possible. 

In the early days of each phase, it will be valuable to know when to go **DOWN** a loop when things go wrong (for reliability)… 

but it will probably be more valuable to know how to go **UP** a loop as models improve (for leverage).

If you don’t figure out how to do this, don’t be salty when you lose to those that do.
remarks.translation: # #在Loopcraft上，人们可能会争辩说，下个世纪的整个游戏都是为了能够尽可能有效地堆叠循环。在每个阶段的早期，当出现问题时，知道什么时候* *向下* *循环是很有价值的（为了可靠性） ……但随着模型的改进（为了杠杆作用） ，知道如何* *向上* *循环可能更有价值。如果你不知道如何做到这一点，当你输给那些这样做的人时，不要咸。
remarks.observation: 这条言论适合观察 builder 对产品、创业、销售或市场节奏的判断；可作为选题线索，不直接等同于行业结论。

remarks.name: Aaron Levie
remarks.role: ceo box - your business lives in content. unleash it with AI
remarks.text:
At Box, we just surveyed 1,640 IT leaders across the US, Japan, and Europe about agentic AI adoption. Many standout findings, but a big one was that the companies that adopted AI the most are planning to grow headcount the most.

Obviously lots of ways you can read that data and variables mixed in, but it’s actually quite intuitive that the companies that become most productive want to (and are able to) reinvest back into the business to keep getting the gains going. 

The narrative of jobs being wiped out assumes that companies will take a fixed approach to what they want to be able for work on. What’s happening in practice is it’s causing companies to want to light up more engineering projects, sell to more customers, automate more processes to give time back, and more. That all leads to more work to be done by people.
remarks.translation:
在Box ，我们刚刚对美国、日本和欧洲的1,640名IT领导者进行了有关AgentAI采用情况的调查。许多突出的发现，但一个重要的发现是，采用AI最多的公司计划增加最多的员工人数。显然，您可以通过多种方式读取数据和变量，但实际上，生产率最高的公司希望（并且能够）重新投资于业务以继续获得收益，这是非常直观的。
工作岗位被淘汰的叙述假设公司将采取一种固定的方法来处理他们希望能够从事的工作。在实践中发生的事情是，它导致公司想要点亮更多的工程项目，向更多的客户销售，自动化更多的流程以节省时间等等。所有这些都会导致人们做更多的工作。
remarks.observation: 这条言论适合观察 builder 对产品、创业、销售或市场节奏的判断；可作为选题线索，不直接等同于行业结论。

remarks.name: Swyx
remarks.role: achieve ambition with intentionality, intensity, integrity & insanity.
remarks.text:
the #1 thing that is driving me to build my own vibecoding platform rn is that none of them - and i lov vercel, cloudflare, netlify etc - none of them really close the loop for you in terms of setting you on the right path with errors and pinging you when shit fails (shit always fails)  

there's way too much "webmaster" infra to setup for every single project and i just want to do it once and for all, instead i'm being asked to npx posthog wizard here and npx arize skills there and it all just needs to be swallowed up into One Thing.
remarks.translation:
驱使我构建自己的振动编码平台rn的第一件事是，他们都没有-我喜欢vercel、cloudflare、netlify等-他们都没有真正为你关闭循环，让你在正确的路径上设置错误，并在大便失败时ping你（大便总是失败） ，每个项目都有太多的“网站管理员”基础设施需要设置，我只想一劳永逸地完成它，相反，我被要求在这里使用npx posthog向导和npx arize sk
那里的弊病，而这一切只需要被吞噬成一件事。
remarks.observation: 这条言论适合观察 builder 对产品、创业、销售或市场节奏的判断；可作为选题线索，不直接等同于行业结论。

remarks.name: Amjad Masad
remarks.role: ceo replit. civilizationist
remarks.text:
This is what building a company looks like on Replit. 

One canvas with your web app, mobile app, marketing & App Store material.

Click into any one of those and start building, changing, and generating new things. https://t.co/gX7qCakTOe
remarks.translation: 这就是在Replit上构建公司的样子。一个画布，包含您的网络应用程序、移动应用程序、营销和App Store材料。点击其中任何一个，开始构建、更改和创建新事物。
remarks.observation: 这条言论适合观察 AI 编程工具、设计到代码流程和软件生产方式的变化；需要结合产品发布或文档再做事实判断。

remarks.name: Amjad Masad
remarks.role: ceo replit. civilizationist
remarks.text:
For the first time, I'm vibecoding with ZERO frustration and in a complete state of flow, so much so that I'm running out of ideas.

Typically, I have so much backlog of things I want to add, but after Fable landed on Replit, I'm almost certain I don't need more IQ for vibecoding, just cheaper and faster models, and we're done here.
remarks.translation: 这是我第一次以零挫败感和完整的流动状态进行振动编码，以至于我失去了想法。通常，我想添加的东西有很多积压，但在Fable登陆Replit之后，我几乎可以肯定我不需要更多的智商来进行振动编码，只需要更便宜、更快的型号，我们就完成了。
remarks.observation: 这条言论适合观察 AI 编程工具、设计到代码流程和软件生产方式的变化；需要结合产品发布或文档再做事实判断。

remarks.name: Peter Steinberger
remarks.role: Polyagentmorous ClawFather. Came back from retirement to mess with AI and help a lobster take over the world.
remarks.text:
Part of the OpenClaw hardening work is reducing surface risk; for some media conversion we had to shell out to ffmpeg.

In the next release that can now be done via wasm, with similar performance for our use cases.
https://t.co/tGOI87C7lc
remarks.translation: OpenClaw硬化工作的一部分是降低表面风险；对于某些媒体转换，我们不得不外包给ffmpeg。在下一个版本中，现在可以通过wasm完成，在我们的用例中具有类似的性能。
remarks.observation: 这条言论可作为 Agent 产品形态、协作方式或能力边界的观察入口；只代表公开观点，不作为公司事实主证据。

podcasts.name: Unsupervised Learning (Redpoint)
podcasts.title: Ep 89: AI Research Legend’s Honest Assessment of Where We Are
podcasts.excerpt: This episode with Lukasz Kaiser, co-author of the seminal "Attention Is All You Need" transformer paper and former researcher at both Google Brain and OpenAI, is a wide-ranging conversation about the fundamental limits of current AI architectures and whether transformers will continue to dominate or eventually give way to something new. Lukasz brings a rare dual perspective: deep belief in how far the current paradigm has taken us (he's an enthusiastic daily Codex user who's seen 10x productivity gains in his own research), while maintaining genuine intellectual humility about whether transformers can truly generalize the way humans do. The episode weaves together questions about data efficiency, the non-verifiable RL frontier, the coding agent revolution, the open vs. closed source gap, and what the next architectural leap might look like: all filtered through the lens of someone who helped bu…

podcasts.name: Latent Space
podcasts.title: 🔬ESM: The Bitter Lesson is Coming for Proteins - Alex Rives, BioHub
podcasts.excerpt: Editor’s note: In our first BioHub pod with Priscilla and Mark they discussed their acquisition of EvoScale, led by Alex Rives, who is now Head of Science at BioHub. With ESM-1 they trained language models on millions of protein sequences drawn from across life, with a simple “next token” objective: predict the amino acids that have been randomly masked out, based on the context of the rest of the sequence. But they soon found that these models also learned biological structure and function, including properties the model had never been explicitly s…

podcasts.name: AI & I by Every
podcasts.title: We Automated Everything With AI and Tripled Our Headcount
podcasts.excerpt: Dan Shipper runs one of the most AI-native companies today. Every has agents embedded in nearly every workflow—“if you swing a stick in our Slack, you're as likely to hit a human as an agent,” he says. And yet the company has grown from four people to 30 since GPT-3 came out, and is still hiring.Why does Dan believe there's more human work to do than ever?In a format flip for AI & I, Every's COO Brandon Gell turns the tables and interviews Dan about his latest essay, “After Automation”—an 8,000-word argument for why rising automation doesn't eliminate demand for human work, it increases it. The thesis: AI makes yesterday's expert competence cheap and widely available, which floods every field with output that's close but not quite right—and that creates more demand for the humans who can take it the rest of the way.Dan talked with Brandon about the paradox at the heart of a…
